title: carex supina
intro: Carex supina, also known as Nuttall's sedge or bottlebrush sedge, is a type of perennial plant native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with roots and rhizomes exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant has been used to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, among other health benefits. Carex supina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to leverage its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
